title: Success State UX for 100% Readiness (Gap Analysis & Learning Plan)

acceptanceCriteria.description: Success banner replaces empty breakdown when no gaps exist
acceptanceCriteria.given: report.gapAnalysis.gaps is empty AND report.gapAnalysis.overallReadiness is 100
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Skill Gap Breakdown section is replaced by a success state with a trophy/success icon, congratulatory heading, and the summary text from gapAnalysis.summary
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty gaps array, assert success icon and heading present, assert 'Skill Gap Breakdown' heading absent

acceptanceCriteria.description: Proficiency scores displayed in success state
acceptanceCriteria.given: report.gapAnalysis.gaps is empty AND report.proficiencyScores is non-empty
acceptanceCriteria.when: the gap analysis page renders in success state
acceptanceCriteria.then: each proficiency score is displayed with skillName, score percentage, and a progress bar
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty gaps and populated proficiencyScores, assert each skill name and score text visible

acceptanceCriteria.description: Next-step CTAs on gap analysis success state
acceptanceCriteria.given: the gap analysis page is in success state
acceptanceCriteria.then: at least two CTAs are visible: navigate to learning plan and start a new assessment
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render success state, assert presence of 'Generate Learning Plan' button and a secondary CTA button

acceptanceCriteria.description: Radar chart still renders in success state
acceptanceCriteria.then: the RadarChart component and GapSummary component still render
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty gaps, assert radar chart and gap summary components present

acceptanceCriteria.description: Normal breakdown shown when gaps exist even with high readiness
acceptanceCriteria.given: report.gapAnalysis.overallReadiness is 95 AND report.gapAnalysis.gaps has 1+ items
acceptanceCriteria.then: the normal gap card breakdown is shown, not the success state
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with high readiness but non-empty gaps array, assert GapCard components rendered

acceptanceCriteria.then: the empty sidebar and PlanTimeline are replaced by a success state with a success icon and mastery message
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty phases array, assert success component present, assert phase navigation absent

acceptanceCriteria.then: the PlanHeader zero-value stats (0 Phases, 0 Hours, 0 Concepts) are hidden or replaced with achievement metrics
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty phases, assert '0 Phases' text not present

acceptanceCriteria.description: Proficiency scores shown on learning plan success state
acceptanceCriteria.given: report.learningPlan.phases is empty AND report.proficiencyScores is non-empty
acceptanceCriteria.when: the learning plan page renders in success state
acceptanceCriteria.then: the user's assessed skills and scores are displayed
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty phases and populated proficiencyScores, assert skill names and scores visible

acceptanceCriteria.description: Next-step CTAs on learning plan success state
acceptanceCriteria.given: the learning plan page is in success state
acceptanceCriteria.then: CTAs for Back to Gap Analysis, Start Over, and Export Report are visible
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render success state, assert presence of Back, Start Over, and Export buttons

acceptanceCriteria.description: Learning plan with phases renders normally
acceptanceCriteria.then: the normal phase navigation, PlanHeader stats, and PlanTimeline render as before
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with populated phases array, assert PlanHeader and PlanTimeline components present

acceptanceCriteria.given: report.gapAnalysis.gaps is empty AND report.proficiencyScores is empty
acceptanceCriteria.when: the gap analysis page renders in success state
acceptanceCriteria.then: the success banner is shown without a skill scores section; no error thrown
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with empty gaps and empty proficiencyScores, assert success icon present, assert no error boundary triggered

acceptanceCriteria.description: Success state via session URL param
acceptanceCriteria.given: user navigates to /gap-analysis?session=<id> where the session has no gaps
acceptanceCriteria.when: the page loads and report is fetched
acceptanceCriteria.then: the success state renders correctly without depending on store targetLevel
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: render page with session search param, empty gaps, no targetLevel in store, assert success renders

acceptanceCriteria.description: RadarChart with empty gaps array (regression guard)
acceptanceCriteria.testStrategy: existing test covers this — verify no regression

edgeCases.expectedBehavior: Show success state; summary text from LLM explains the nuance
Show normal gap card breakdown (gaps exist)
Success state renders single skill card; no layout issues
Skill list scrolls or wraps; no overflow

edgeCases.scenario: learningPlan.phases empty AND summary is empty string
edgeCases.expectedBehavior: Default mastery message shown instead of empty text
'Try a harder level' CTA hidden; show 'Explore other roles' or 'Add more skills' instead
Use generic message instead of level-specific text

performance.networkRequests: 0 additional API calls for success state

accessibility.screenReader: success banner uses role=status or aria-live=polite
accessibility.colorIndependence: icon + text convey success independently of color

reliability.noUnhandledErrors: success state must not throw for any valid API response shape

doneConditions: Gap analysis page renders success state when gapAnalysis.gaps is empty
Success state on gap analysis shows celebratory icon and heading
Success state on gap analysis displays proficiency scores from report
Success state on gap analysis provides next-step CTAs
Radar chart and GapSummary render correctly in success state
Learning plan page renders success state when learningPlan.phases is empty
Success state on learning plan hides or replaces zero-value stats
Success state on learning plan displays proficiency scores
Success state on learning plan provides next-step CTAs
Edge cases handled: empty proficiencyScores, missing targetLevel, single skill
Success state is accessible: keyboard navigable, screen reader friendly
New unit tests cover success state rendering for both pages
make check passes (lint + typecheck + test + build)
Demo report page unaffected by changes

reuseCandidates.action: Extract shared SkillScoreCard component or duplicate pattern

reuseCandidates.reuse: Layout pattern: icon + heading + description + CTA
reuseCandidates.action: Follow the pattern for success state components

conflictFlags.risk: Direct modification of gap card rendering section
May need conditional rendering; could affect demo report page

openQuestions.question: Should we extract a shared SkillScoreCard component from AssessmentComplete or duplicate the ~20 lines in each success state?
openQuestions.recommendation: Extract shared component — 3 consumers (AssessmentComplete, gap-analysis success, learning-plan success)

openQuestions.question: When targetLevel is 'staff' (highest), what CTA replaces 'Try a harder level'?
openQuestions.recommendation: Show 'Explore other roles' or 'Add more skills' instead

openQuestions.question: Should success state trigger on gaps.length === 0 or overallReadiness === 100?
openQuestions.recommendation: Trigger on gaps.length === 0 — this is the condition that causes the empty UI

openQuestions.question: Should PlanHeader be modified to handle empty phases or should the page render a different component?
openQuestions.recommendation: Page conditionally renders a separate success component instead of PlanHeader + PlanTimeline
